description: Known false-positive senders / patterns for the SA V5 order classifier (order_monitor.py). Each entry captures a junk SD that was created by the classifier in error, with the sender signature and reason for rejection. order_monitor.py loads this file and uses sender_patterns as an exclusion list.
source_incident: SA V5 D.10 — three junk SDs created from April 16 emails (Carlos Zapata, edevans@eufmc.com, GHK Admin). Root cause: the line 72 Gmail query used overly broad from: filters, and the classifier's signal check allowed generic keywords (e.g. 'order') to pass without domain verification.

examples.reason: Classifier matched 'zapata' token in CUSTOMER_DOMAINS list but sender has no relationship to Norris Utilities — no order-signal keywords, no PO, no product references. Common first/last name caused false positive.
EUFMC (Electric Utility Fleet Managers Conference) is an industry event; the sender is a conference/mailing-list address, not a customer. No order-signal keywords present.

Unknown admin sender unrelated to Norris Utilities — no order-signal keywords, no PO, no product references. 'GHK' token matched nothing in the known-customer list, meaning the email passed via a signal-based query but never had strong signals.

min_confidence_policy.rule: If the sender is NOT in CUSTOMER_DOMAINS (unknown sender) AND the email's subject+snippet contains NO strong_signal_keyword, the classifier MUST NOT create an SD. Log and skip the thread.
min_confidence_policy.backward_compatible: Existing orders already in shipping_docs.json and pending_orders.json are NOT re-processed. This policy only affects new, unseen thread IDs.
min_confidence_policy.escalation: If a legitimate sender is rejected by this policy, Aaron/CB adds them to CUSTOMER_DOMAINS (order_monitor.py line 60-65) and the next scan picks them up.

gmail_query_exclusions.description: NOT from: tokens to add to the known-customers-by-name query on order_monitor.py line 72, so junk addresses are filtered before classification.
